page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m2.large is a burstable instance type belonging to the ecs.t6 family. It features 2 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps under a KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance characteristics. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1300.19, and the multi-core score is 2402.69, both placing in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show 186.46 GB/sec for cached small blocks, but this drops to 20.68 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, which is in the bottom 10%. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 1045.0 and a multi-core score of 1850.0. Database operations under Passmark score 677.83, and Redis SET operations achieve 2.57 million operations per second.

Given its burstable CPU allocation and modest hardware profile, this instance offers a cost-effective solution for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that experience occasional spikes. It is not designed for sustained high-performance computing, large-scale database hosting, or memory-intensive tasks. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, development and testing environments, code repositories, and simple microservices that can operate within the 4.0 GB memory limit and burstable CPU credit system.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m2.large is a burstable instance within the ecs.t6 family, configured with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates entry-level performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1045.0 and a multi-core score of 1850.0, alongside a Passmark database score of 677.83. Due to its burstable CPU allocation, this instance is highly cost-efficient for low-intensity workloads that experience intermittent activity. It is best suited for development environments, lightweight web servers, and small-scale microservices.

tagline: A budget-friendly burstable instance with dual vCPUs and entry-level memory for lightweight, intermittent workloads and development environments.
